title: Request Header Modification
description:
## Request Header Modification

Gateway API defines standard HTTP filters to modify requests or responses without needing custom ingress annotations. The `RequestHeaderModifier` filter allows adding, setting, or removing headers before forwarding the request to the backend service.

**Your task:**

Create an `HTTPRoute` named `headers-route` in the `gateway-lab` namespace that satisfies the following conditions:
- Attaches to the parent Gateway `external-gateway` in the `gateway-lab` namespace.
- Matches incoming traffic with path prefix `/headers`.
- Applies a `RequestHeaderModifier` filter to set or add the HTTP header `X-Gateway-Route` with the value `external-route`.
- Routes the modified traffic to the backend service `headers-service` on port `80`.

```bash
# Verify your progress:
kubectl get httproute headers-route -n gateway-lab -o yaml
```

hints.title: HTTPRoute with filters
hints.body: In HTTPRoute, filters can be defined under spec.rules[].filters. Use a filter with type: RequestHeaderModifier and configure the requestHeaderModifier block with set (or add).
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: HTTPRoute
metadata:
  name: headers-route
  namespace: gateway-lab
spec:
  parentRefs:
  - name: external-gateway
  rules:
  - matches:
    - path:
        type: PathPrefix
        value: /headers
    filters:
    - type: RequestHeaderModifier
      requestHeaderModifier:
        set:
        - name: X-Gateway-Route
          value: external-route
    backendRefs:
    - name: headers-service
      port: 80
EOF

setup_commands.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: GatewayClass
metadata:
  name: shared-gateway-class
spec:
  controllerName: example.com/gateway-controller
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: external-gateway
  namespace: gateway-lab
spec:
  gatewayClassName: shared-gateway-class
  listeners:
  - name: http-listener
    port: 80
    protocol: HTTP
    allowedRoutes:
      namespaces:
        from: Same
EOF
kubectl create deployment headers-service --image=nginx:alpine -n gateway-lab 2>/dev/null || true
kubectl expose deployment headers-service --port=80 -n gateway-lab 2>/dev/null || true

validation.description: Check that the HTTPRoute headers-route is configured with the header modifier filter

commands.command: kubectl get httproute headers-route -n gateway-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'

commands.description: Filter sets/adds the header value to external-route
Backend service is headers-service on port 80
